Blog Writeups About

    Recreate, detect and exploit the Log4j vulnerability

    CVE Log4j PoC

    Log4j is a widely used logging library in Java. You use it to log all sort of things such as network traffic, requests, responses, application operational logs, etc... Imagine any of those data this library processes can let hackers penetrate your system. That's what we are dealing with.

    11 July 2022

    Bypass SSL Pinning in Flutter using IDA

    Android

    Flutter is a free and open-source mobile UI framework. Hackers and penetration testers can bump into some problems with certificates and ssl pinning when testing applications built with Flutter. This article is a detailed guide to solve all that bs.

    21 March 2022

    Handling HTTP signed requests with Burp Suite

    Signature Burp Suite Android

    Many Android applications include signatures for requests nowadays, making scanning and repeating requests difficult. Fortunately, there is a way to deal with this issue in Burp using an extension called Cyber Security Transformation Chef (CSTC).
